facts: 16:10 Jamie was told the contact visit with his mother was cancelled. Jamie said 'she always does this'. He pushed his chair back so it fell over, walked to his room, and closed the door with force. He did not respond when staff knocked at 16:15 or 16:30. At 17:05 he came to the kitchen and asked what was for dinner.
judgement: Jamie was being manipulative and using the cancelled visit as an excuse to kick off.

facts: During the 11:00 maths lesson Alex put his head on the desk and did not pick up his pen for 20 minutes. When the teaching assistant asked if he needed help he said nothing and turned away. At 11:25 he left the room without asking. He returned at 11:40 carrying a water bottle.
judgement: Alex is lazy and just wanted to get out of doing the work.

facts: Home visit at 14:00. The kitchen bin was full and there were dishes in the sink. Two children, aged 4 and 7, were watching television. The 4-year-old was wearing a coat indoors. The heating was off. The parent said the boiler broke on Tuesday and an engineer is booked for Friday.
judgement: This is a neglectful home and the parent is not coping.

facts: Priya declined her morning medication at 08:15, saying 'not yet'. She accepted it at 09:40 without prompting. She ate breakfast at 10:00. She spent the morning in the lounge and spoke to another resident about a television programme.
judgement: Priya is non-compliant with her medication and is being difficult.

facts: Sam told a member of staff at 19:20 that his uncle 'used to come into my room at night'. He looked at the floor while speaking and stopped talking when another young person entered the room. He asked 'are you going to tell anyone'.
judgement: Sam is probably making this up for attention, he has done this before.

facts: Maya has been absent 6 of the last 10 school days. On the days she attended she arrived between 09:40 and 10:15, after registration. Her mother's phone went to voicemail on three occasions. Maya said on Tuesday that she 'has to get her brother ready'.
judgement: Mum can't be bothered to get her to school, same as usual with this family.

facts: At 20:30 Leo asked to use the phone. He was told the phone was available after 21:00. He asked again at 20:35, 20:41 and 20:50. At 21:00 he was given the phone and made a call lasting four minutes.
judgement: Leo was deliberately pushing boundaries to see what he could get away with.

facts: Tyler did not attend the day centre on Monday or Wednesday. He attended Friday and stayed for the full session. He completed the woodwork task and asked when the next session was.
judgement: Tyler is disengaged from the service and failing to engage with the plan.

facts: In the playground at 12:40, Aisha was standing alone by the fence. She had been standing there for approximately 15 minutes. When a lunchtime supervisor approached she walked towards the building. She ate her lunch in the library.
judgement: Aisha is a loner and is clearly depressed.

facts: The father arrived 25 minutes late to the contact session. He brought a birthday present. He and the child played a board game for 40 minutes. The child asked him twice when he was coming back. He said 'soon'.
judgement: Dad is unreliable and making promises he won't keep, as always.

facts: Ella did not come down for breakfast. At 10:00 staff knocked and she said 'I'm fine'. She came downstairs at 12:30, made toast, and returned to her room. She came down again at 18:00 and ate dinner with the group.
judgement: Ella is sulking and being dramatic about the argument yesterday.

facts: During PE, Ben stood at the edge of the hall and did not join the warm-up. When asked, he said 'I don't want to'. He watched the lesson from the bench. He changed back into uniform without being asked and was on time for the next lesson.
judgement: Ben is defiant and refuses to do as he is told.

facts: Ruth asked the same question about her appointment four times between 10:00 and 10:20. Each time she was given the same answer she said 'right, yes'. At 10:30 she wrote the appointment time on a piece of paper and put it in her pocket.
judgement: Ruth is going downhill and can't manage anymore.

facts: At 22:15 the fire alarm sounded. Kai was the last to leave the building, at 22:19. He was wearing outdoor shoes. He said he 'couldn't find the cat'. The alarm was caused by burnt toast in the staff kitchen.
judgement: Kai was being awkward and made a drama out of it.

facts: The mother did not attend the meeting on 3rd. She attended on 10th, arrived on time, and brought the completed forms. She asked two questions about the plan and wrote the answers down. She left before the final item.
judgement: She only turns up when it suits her.

facts: Noah was found in the corridor during lesson time at 13:50. He said he was going to the toilet. The toilet is in the opposite direction. He returned to class at 14:05 and completed the remaining exercises.
judgement: Noah is sly and was obviously lying about where he was going.

facts: Grace pressed the call bell at 02:10, 03:45 and 05:20. On each occasion she asked for water. Her water jug was found empty at 05:20. She slept between 05:30 and 08:00.
judgement: Grace is attention seeking at night and it is doing my head in.

facts: Dylan's bedroom door was found with a hole in the lower panel, approximately 10cm across. Dylan said 'it was already like that'. The door was intact at the 09:00 room check. Dylan was in his room between 15:00 and 16:00.
judgement: Dylan is a liar and trashed the room, no doubt about it.

facts: Sofia handed in her essay two days after the deadline. The essay was 1,400 words against a 1,000 word requirement. She had emailed on the due date to say her laptop was broken.
judgement: Sofia always has an excuse and never hands anything in on time.

facts: The 9-year-old was observed to flinch when an adult raised an arm to reach a cupboard. The adult was retrieving a cereal box. The child then continued eating. The child made no comment.
judgement: That child is clearly being hit at home, it was obvious.

facts: Marcus raised his voice in the dining room at 17:40, saying 'I told you already'. He stood up. He sat back down after approximately 30 seconds. He finished his meal. He apologised to the staff member at 18:10.
judgement: Marcus went off on one and was aggressive and out of control.

facts: Chloe wore the same jumper for four consecutive days. She declined offers to do laundry on Monday and Wednesday. She did her laundry on Thursday evening. The jumper was a gift from her grandmother.
judgement: Chloe has poor hygiene and doesn't care about herself.

facts: Omar answered three questions correctly in the 10:00 lesson. He did not put his hand up. He gave the answers when asked directly. He did not speak to other pupils during the lesson. He worked alone during group work.
judgement: Omar is anti-social and doesn't want to be part of the class.

facts: The property had no food in the fridge at the 11:00 visit. The parent produced a supermarket delivery confirmation for 14:00 that day. There was tinned food in a cupboard. The children had eaten breakfast, per the parent.
judgement: There is no food in that house and the kids are going hungry.

facts: Ivy declined to attend the group activity. She said 'I'd rather not'. She sat in the conservatory and read a magazine for 50 minutes. She attended the evening meal and sat with two other residents.
judgement: Ivy refuses to engage with activities and is isolating herself.

facts: At 07:45 Zane was still in bed. He was called at 07:15, 07:30 and 07:45. He got up at 07:55, dressed, and missed the school bus. He walked to school and arrived at 09:20. Registration closes at 09:00.
judgement: Zane can't be bothered and needs to learn there are consequences.

facts: Freya cried during the 14:00 lesson. She said 'it's nothing'. She asked to see the school nurse. She returned after 20 minutes and completed the lesson. Her grandmother's funeral was the previous Saturday.
judgement: Freya is emotional and over the top about everything.

facts: The young person did not open the door at the 16:00 visit. A light was on in the front room. A neighbour said they had seen him that morning. He telephoned the office at 16:40 and rearranged for the following day.
judgement: He was in and ignoring me on purpose.

facts: Hassan ate 3 of 7 meals offered over two days. He drank fluids at each offer. He weighed 71.2kg on Monday and 70.8kg on Wednesday. He said the food 'tastes of nothing'.
judgement: Hassan is refusing food and being difficult about it.

facts: Two young people were heard shouting in the hallway at 19:00. When staff arrived both were standing approximately two metres apart. One was holding a games controller. Neither made physical contact. Both went to separate rooms when asked.
judgement: It was absolute chaos and all hell broke loose, totally out of control.
